intro: Ideleria neesii, also known as Ideleria, is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, native to Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ideleria neesii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Ideleria is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and its extracts are used in traditional medicine and phytotherapy, particularly in Brazil and other South American countries.
